lessons: In Preview, deleting a PDF page requires: (1) ensure thumbnail sidebar is visible, (2) click the target page thumbnail to select it, (3) press Delete key, (4) save with Cmd+S. This 3-keystroke sequence is reliable.
Preview's thumbnail sidebar is often already visible when opening PDFs; check before attempting to enable it to avoid unnecessary steps.
effectiveStrategies: Using the thumbnail sidebar for visual page selection ensures the correct page is targeted before deletion.
Verifying success via the page count change (20→19) and absence of the '— Edited' title bar indicator provided reliable confirmation of both delete and save operations.

lessons: In macOS Preview, use Tools menu → 'Adjust Colour...' (or shortcut Alt+Cmd+C) to access brightness, contrast, and other image adjustment sliders.
When dragging sliders in adjustment panels, use left_click_drag starting from the current slider position and move horizontally to increase/decrease values.
effectiveStrategies: Using the keyboard shortcut Alt+Cmd+C to open the Adjust Colour panel directly after accessing the Tools menu
Identifying checkpoints at each major milestone (menu open, brightness adjusted, contrast adjusted) to confirm progress before proceeding

After activating a selection tool in a GUI app, always perform the actual selection drag action before attempting menu operations; tool activation alone does not constitute task progress.

For image manipulation tasks, verify each sub-step produces a visible artifact (e.g., a drawn selection rectangle) before proceeding to the next step, rather than assuming tool activation implies readiness.

The crop operation (Tools > Crop or Cmd+K) was never executed
